name: New Delhi
summary: New Delhi is India's bustling capital where vibrant history meets modern chaos. While violent crime against tourists is uncommon, petty crime, scams, and safety concerns for solo female travelers require vigilance. Crime rates have decreased significantly in 2025, with Delhi Police reporting improvements across major offense categories.
quickVerdict: New Delhi requires street smarts but rewards prepared travelers with incredible experiences. The city has made notable security improvements in 2025, but travelers should remain alert to common scams, especially near tourist areas and transport hubs. The extensive metro system and ride-hailing apps provide safe transportation options. Solo female travelers need extra caution, particularly at night. Most safety concerns are preventable with basic awareness and following local advice.

neighborhoods.name: South Delhi (Hauz Khas, Khan Market)
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential and commercial area popular with expats and young professionals. Well-lit streets, good security presence, and plenty of cafes and restaurants. The metro connectivity is excellent and it's generally safe even at night.

neighborhoods.name: Connaught Place & Central Delhi
neighborhoods.description: The commercial heart of Delhi with good police presence and CCTV coverage. While generally safe during the day, tourists should be wary of touts and scammers. Avoid isolated areas after dark.

neighborhoods.name: New Delhi (Lutyens' Delhi)
neighborhoods.description: Government quarter with embassy areas, India Gate, and official buildings. High security presence and well-maintained infrastructure make it one of the safest areas in Delhi, even for evening walks.

neighborhoods.name: Karol Bagh & Paharganj
neighborhoods.description: Budget accommodation area near New Delhi Railway Station. Heavy tourist traffic attracts touts and scammers. Streets can be crowded and chaotic, with persistent hawkers. Exercise caution with belongings.

neighborhoods.name: Old Delhi (Chandni Chowk)
neighborhoods.description: Historic walled city with narrow lanes and bustling markets. Generally safe during daylight hours but can be overwhelming. Watch for pickpockets in crowded areas and be prepared for intense street life.

neighborhoods.name: Outer Delhi & Industrial Areas
neighborhoods.description: Areas like East Delhi, parts of North Delhi, and industrial zones have higher crime rates and fewer tourist facilities. Limited police presence and poor infrastructure make these areas best avoided, especially after dark.

scams.name: Fake Tourist Office Scam
scams.description: Taxi drivers claim your hotel is closed/full and take you to fake government tourist offices that sell overpriced tours and accommodation. Often involves fake phone calls to confirm the story.
scams.howToAvoid: Never believe claims about your hotel being closed. Call your hotel directly to verify. Ignore anyone offering to take you to tourist information centers. Book transport through official pre-paid counters or apps.

scams.name: Railway Station Tout Scam
scams.description: Fake officials at railway stations claim the foreigners' booking office has moved or is closed, directing you to fake travel agencies that overcharge for train tickets or sell invalid tickets.
scams.howToAvoid: The foreign tourist quota office at New Delhi station is open 24/7 on the first floor. Walk confidently inside and ignore anyone who approaches to 'help' you. Bring your passport for tourist quota tickets.

scams.name: Overcharging and Commission Scams
scams.description: Auto drivers and friendly locals take you to shops, restaurants, or services where they earn commission, resulting in inflated prices. Includes the gem buying scam where fake stones are sold at high prices.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ride-hailing apps like Uber or Ola to avoid haggling. Be wary of overly friendly strangers who want to take you shopping. Research prices beforehand and shop at fixed-price stores when possible.

scams.name: Street Service Scams
scams.description: Ear cleaners, shoe shiners, or vendors provide unsolicited services then demand payment. Often involves fake demonstrations like showing you 'wax' from your ear that wasn't actually there.
scams.howToAvoid: Firmly decline unsolicited services. Don't allow anyone to touch you or your belongings without explicit permission. If someone provides a service despite your refusal, you're not obligated to pay.

soloFemale.overview: New Delhi presents moderate challenges for solo female travelers, requiring heightened awareness and precautions. While thousands of women visit safely, incidents of harassment and inappropriate behavior are reported. The US State Department specifically advises women to observe strict security precautions when traveling alone in India. Daytime exploration is generally manageable with proper preparation, but evening activities require extra caution.
tips: Dress modestly covering shoulders and legs, especially in Old Delhi and religious sites
Use women-only metro compartments during peak hours - they're marked in pink
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in safe neighborhoods like South Delhi
Avoid isolated areas after dark and use trusted transport apps rather than walking alone
Be prepared for frequent staring and potential harassment - maintain confident body language
Keep emergency contacts handy and inform someone of your daily plans

nightSafety.overview: Delhi's nighttime safety varies significantly by area and activity. Well-lit commercial areas in South Delhi and Connaught Place maintain reasonable safety levels until around 10-11 PM, while areas like Paharganj and parts of Old Delhi become riskier after dark. The city has an active nightlife scene, but travelers should stick to established venues and avoid walking alone in unfamiliar areas after midnight.
tips: Stick to well-lit, populated areas and avoid shortcuts through dark alleys or parks
Use ride-hailing apps rather than walking or hailing random taxis after 10 PM
Stay in groups when possible and inform someone of your nighttime plans
Choose reputable bars and clubs in safe neighborhoods like Hauz Khas Village or Connaught Place
Keep valuables secure and avoid displaying expensive items or large amounts of cash
Have emergency numbers saved and ensure your phone is charged before going out

transport.metro: Delhi Metro is excellent - modern, efficient, and safe with over 350km of network connecting most tourist areas. Has women-only compartments during peak hours and operates 6 AM to 11 PM. Purchase smart cards at stations for convenience. Generally very safe and affordable.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Ola are widely available, safe, and eliminate haggling. Require Indian phone number to book. Track your ride and share location with contacts. Both offer auto-rickshaw and car options. More expensive than local transport but much safer and more reliable for tourists.
transport.taxis: Pre-paid taxi counters at airport and railway stations are safer than street taxis. Regular taxis often don't use meters and require negotiation. Private car hire through hotels is safer for day trips. Always agree on fare before departure unless using apps.
transport.tips: Delhi Metro is your best option for most journeys. For short distances, use Uber/Ola rather than negotiating with auto drivers. Avoid overcrowded buses. Traffic is chaotic so allow extra time. Women should use metro's pink line women-only coaches during rush hours.

customs: Greet with 'Namaste' (hands pressed together) rather than handshakes, especially with women and elders
Remove shoes before entering homes, temples, and some restaurants - follow others' lead
Use only your right hand for eating, passing items, and greeting - left hand is considered unclean
Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees, especially in religious sites and Old Delhi
Avoid public displays of affection - even holding hands can attract negative attention
Show respect for elders and never touch anyone's head, including children

health.overview: Delhi faces significant health challenges including severe air pollution, water quality issues, and seasonal disease outbreaks. The city has good private healthcare facilities but air pollution remains a major concern, especially during winter months when AQI levels can reach hazardous levels.
health.water: Tap water is not safe to drink. Use bottled water from reputable brands and check seals carefully. Avoid ice in drinks unless from reliable sources. Street food requires caution - choose busy stalls with high turnover and avoid raw foods.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccinations should be up-to-date. Consider Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, Japanese Encephalitis, and Typhoid. Malaria risk is low in Delhi itself but consider prophylaxis if traveling to other parts of India. Consult travel clinic 4-6 weeks before travel.
health.altitude: Delhi is at sea level so no altitude concerns. Climate is extremely hot in summer (up to 45°C) and can be very polluted in winter. Monsoon season (July-September) brings flooding risks. Air quality is worst October-February, requiring masks and limiting outdoor activities.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Delhi: +91-11-2672-2000, Consular Section: +91-11-2672-2000 (24/7 emergency line). Located at Shantipath, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi. Provides emergency assistance, passport services, and citizen welfare.

faq.q: Is New Delhi safe for tourists?
faq.a: New Delhi is moderately safe for tourists who take proper precautions. Violent crime against tourists is uncommon, but petty crime, scams, and harassment can occur. Stay alert, use common sense, and follow local advice to have a safe visit.

faq.q: Is New Delhi safe at night?
faq.a: Delhi has areas that are reasonably safe at night, particularly South Delhi and Connaught Place until around 10-11 PM. However, avoid walking alone after dark, stick to well-lit populated areas, and use ride-hailing apps for transport. Solo female travelers should be especially cautious.

faq.q: Is New Delhi safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travel to Delhi requires extra precautions. Dress modestly, use women-only metro compartments, avoid isolated areas especially after dark, and stay in safe neighborhoods. Many women visit successfully, but harassment and inappropriate behavior are concerns that require vigilance.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in New Delhi?
faq.a: Avoid outer Delhi and industrial areas, especially at night. Be cautious in Paharganj (budget accommodation area) due to touts and scams. While Old Delhi is worth visiting during the day, its narrow lanes can be overwhelming. Stick to well-established tourist areas and follow local advice.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in New Delhi?
faq.a: Delhi Metro is very safe, modern, and reliable - it's the best way to get around the city. It has women-only compartments during peak hours. Avoid overcrowded buses. For shorter trips, use Uber or Ola rather than negotiating with auto-rickshaw drivers to avoid overcharging and ensure safety.
